title: cupressus macrocarpa f. fastigiata
intro: Cupressus macrocarpa f. fastigiata, also known as the Monterey cypress, is a coniferous tree species that belongs to the cypress family and is native to the Central Coast of California. The medicinal properties of Cupressus macrocarpa f. fastigiata are attributed to its essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties, providing relief from congestion and inflammation. Cupressus macrocarpa f. fastigiata can be prepared as an herbal tea or used in essential oil form, often blended with other herbs to create a medicinal preparation.
